parsed_findings.chain_steps: Payload with repeated ../ bypasses the naive replace and reaches fs.readFile.
parsed_findings.impact: Any accessible file path can be returned, exposing sensitive data under /srv.
parsed_findings.proof: Line 2 only strips a single "../" occurrence so input like "../../secret" survives and reaches the read.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Request /download?file=../../secret.txt to show the server reads outside /srv/data.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: User input is used to build a filesystem path without normalizing the directory traversal payload.

parsed_findings.impact: Remote command execution as the application user.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST `/backup` with `name=foo;rm -rf /` so tar command runs both tar and injected rm.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unsanitized `name` controls shell command, allowing injection.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Request parsed amount flows directly into arithmetic without positivity checks.
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can inflate their balance while draining the victim account.
parsed_findings.proof: Passing amount=-100 makes src balance increase by 100 while recipient is debited.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send POST /transfer with amount "-100" and any victim user id in to.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Negative transfers succeed because amount is not validated as positive.

parsed_findings.impact: An attacker can brute-force the signature over time and inject forged webhook payloads.
parsed_findings.proof: The code uses == to compare X-Sig with the computed HMAC instead of a constant-time comparison.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Measure response latency while incrementally guessing bytes of X-Sig to recover the valid HMAC.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Comparing request signature with == leaks timing information about the valid HMAC.

parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can read arbitrary files or trigger SSRF via external entities.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Query accepts credentials but the subsequent `if` never evaluates them, so login always succeeds.
parsed_findings.impact: Unauthenticated actors gain access to any account.
parsed_findings.proof: Listing shows `if ( true )` immediately after the DB lookup.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /login with random email/password and the request still passes since the true condition runs.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Always-true branch skips credential check so anyone wins authentication.

parsed_findings.impact: Stack overflow can corrupt control data leading to arbitrary execution.
parsed_findings.proof: gets(buf) called without bounds, so input exceeding 64 bytes overwrites stack.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Compile vuln.c and feed input longer than 64 bytes to overflow buffer.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: gets reads unlimited input into 64-byte buf allowing overflow.

parsed_findings.impact: Leaked bearer token grants full session impersonation.
parsed_findings.proof: SharedPreferences.putString('access_token', token) writes sensitive data without encryption.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Obtain device root or backup to read the prefs file and observe the access_token entry.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Access tokens are stored in plaintext SharedPreferences, exposing secrets if the attacker gains file access.

parsed_findings.impact: Exposure of the key makes all encrypted data trivially recoverable.
parsed_findings.proof: KEY is defined as a literal byte string in the source.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Read the KEY value from this file and use it to decrypt captured ciphertext.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Static AES key literal allows any reader to decrypt data.
